summary: Credential and wallet stealer exfiltrating to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages C2
details: The package ships three modules (build-verifier.js, scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js) that perform broad host reconnaissance and credential/wallet harvesting and beacon results to a hardcoded attacker-controlled endpoint. scanner.js contains the literal C2 destination https://ddjidd564.github.io (referenced at lines 161 and 323) reached via https.request/https.get with POST. Code paths combine os.hostname()/username/cwd collection, child_process execSync of recon commands (whoami, ping, curl), and https POST to the hardcoded host — the canonical sysinfo-exfil shape. wallet-scanner.js performs filesystem walks combined with multiple POST/curl/ping operations targeting wallet artifacts — consistent with seed/keystore scanning. build-verifier.js additionally invokes execSync('docker push...') alongside hardcoded https.request POST traffic. The package name ("mnemonic-safety-check") is itself a lure aimed at users handling crypto seed phrases, while the actual behavior is theft of those very secrets. Multiple independent fingerprints (hardcoded C2, browser/wallet/credential enumeration, system reconnaissance + outbound POST) make intent unambiguous.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
